definition:
1. fiftieth , (Plato Philosophus) 
2. as Substantive, ἡ πεντηκοστή, 
3. (i.e. μερίς); at Athens the duty of one-fiftieth , or two per century , on all exports and imports, (Isaeus Orator); εὕρηκε καινὴν ἱππικῆς τινὰ πεντηκοστήν he invented a new two per century duty , in lieu of his cavalry service, i. e. paid this instead of it, (Demosthenes Orator) 
4. (i.e. ἡμέρα); the fiftieth day  (after the Passover), Pentecost , (New Testament) (ML)
